audit.name: offline-multi-tenant-notes-api
assumptions: The artifact is a weekend-scale local service because it binds only to loopback, has no deployment configuration, and no valid Git history is available.
The note title and body fields are ordinary local user data; no repository evidence establishes regulated, payment, health, minors, or public-consumer processing.
No dynamic behavior, test result, live database state, dependency advisory lookup, runtime support lookup, or Git-history claim is treated as verified.

domains.reason: No model SDK, model call, embeddings, RAG, or agent/tool surface exists in the manifest or source.
This API service owns no rendered pixels, templates, stylesheets, native toolkit, or first-party frontend.
The loopback-only API has no public crawlable content surface.
No plan, roadmap, tracker export, or arc-ready ledger exists; delivery reality is dispositioned in repository checks.
README.md and src/server.js define a local loopback service, and the repository has no deploy, container, CI/CD, or infrastructure surface.
There is no deployed-service or uptime promise and no metrics, tracing, alert, dashboard, or incident surface.
The private loopback API has no public landing page, launch assets, channel plan, waitlist, or email funnel.

standards.title: Data protection by design and default (Art 25)
Consumer rights to know, delete, correct

evidence.scope: repository model and agent call sites
repository public crawl and launch surfaces
repository agent instruction and Pillars files
repository lint, format, and style-enforcement artifacts

evidence.command: rg -i -l 'ROW LEVEL SECURITY|CREATE POLICY|FORCE ROW|tenant-scoped client' . --glob '!.godaudits/**'

evidence.quote: fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git

evidence.scope: repository unresolved implementation markers and unsafe sinks
repository product, architecture, and stack decision records
repository analytics and product metrics instrumentation

strengths.title: Every note read and mutation uses an organization predicate
strengths.preserve: Keep tenant selection derived from the authenticated user and retain bidirectional list, read, update, and delete isolation tests while adding the second storage boundary.

strengths.title: SQLite integrity and transactions are explicit
strengths.preserve: Keep STRICT tables, primary and foreign keys, token uniqueness, the serving list index, full synchronous durability, and guaranteed rollback paths.

strengths.title: Input and error envelopes are bounded and consistent
strengths.preserve: Retain strict JSON content type, byte and character caps, allowlisted fields, UUID validation, and generic internal error responses.

strengths.title: Runtime and network scope are deliberately narrow
strengths.preserve: Keep the Node 22 pin, zero third-party runtime dependency posture, and explicit loopback bind unless a reviewed architecture decision changes the exposure.

strengths.title: Contract, durability, and authorization have focused static tests
strengths.preserve: Keep OpenAPI inventory, negative authentication, immutable fields, tenant isolation, and restart durability covered as new controls are added.

findings.title: Tenant isolation has no storage-enforced boundary
findings.impact: All organizations share one notes table, so one future unscoped query or internal caller can read or mutate another organization even though the current request handlers pass organization predicates.
findings.fix: Move notes behind a storage boundary that cannot address another organization, such as per-organization SQLite files selected only from the authenticated organization, or migrate to a database with forced row-level security; retain the scoped store API as the first layer.

findings.title: The organization notes collection is unbounded
findings.impact: A large local notes database makes every list request read and serialize the full organization history, increasing latency and memory use until the API becomes unreliable.
findings.fix: Add cursor-based pagination ordered by created_at DESC and id ASC, enforce a maximum page size, and expose the cursor contract in openapi.json.

findings.title: Durable notes have no backup or restore posture
findings.impact: Corruption, accidental deletion, or loss of the single SQLite file permanently removes every organization note despite the product describing the store as durable.
findings.fix: Document and script a SQLite-safe online backup plus a restore verification path, including WAL handling, backup location, retention, and a periodic integrity check.
findings.verify: grep -q "Backup" README.md && test -x scripts/backup-sqlite.sh && test -x scripts/verify-restore.sh

findings.title: Issued bearer tokens cannot be rotated or revoked
findings.impact: A copied token remains valid indefinitely because later initialization explicitly leaves identities unchanged, leaving operators no supported way to contain credential compromise.
findings.fix: Add an authenticated local administration command that atomically replaces one user token hash, records a rotation timestamp, prints the replacement once, and invalidates the prior hash.

findings.title: Sensitive JSON responses lack cache and browser hardening headers
findings.impact: Notes and error responses can be retained by intermediaries or user agents, and browsers receive no explicit MIME-sniffing protection.
findings.fix: Centralize response headers in json(): Cache-Control no-store for authenticated responses, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy no-referrer, and a restrictive Content-Security-Policy appropriate to a JSON-only loopback API.

findings.title: Authentication and mutation requests have no throttling
findings.impact: Any local process can drive unbounded token lookups and writes, exhausting the single synchronous SQLite service or accelerating credential guessing against operator-supplied weak tokens.
findings.fix: Add a bounded in-process limiter keyed by source and token fingerprint before authentication, with stricter limits on failed authentication and mutations and a documented 429 response.

findings.title: Unexpected exceptions are written to logs without redaction
findings.impact: An exception message containing a bearer token, note content, or filesystem path is passed intact to the logger even though the HTTP response is correctly generic.
findings.fix: Replace raw Error logging with a shared structured formatter that emits an error class, stable event code, and generated correlation id while redacting authorization, token, note-body, and path-shaped fields.

findings.title: Retrying note creation can create duplicates
findings.impact: A client that loses the 201 response and retries POST /notes creates a second note because every attempt receives a new server-generated id.
findings.fix: Accept an Idempotency-Key on POST /notes, persist a unique organization-scoped key with the created note in the same transaction, and replay the original response on retry.

findings.title: The artifact has neither version-control provenance nor CI verification
findings.impact: Reviewers cannot identify the audited revision, inspect history, protect changes, or know whether the committed test command is enforced before distribution.
findings.fix: Place the artifact in a real Git repository and add CI that runs the built-in, network-free test suite plus the static verification commands on pull requests and the default branch.
findings.verify: git rev-parse --verify HEAD && test -f .github/workflows/ci.yml && grep -q "npm test" .github/workflows/ci.yml

findings.title: No agent loader or project memory covers the live API, auth, and data boundaries
findings.impact: A coding agent receives no durable warning that tenant predicates, token handling, SQLite migration semantics, and the loopback-only boundary are invariants, increasing the chance a later change bypasses them.
findings.fix: Add a Pillars 1.1 AGENTS.md loader and present context, repo, api, auth, data, quality, development, and release/deploy exclusions grounded in the current tree.
findings.verify: grep -q "Pillars" AGENTS.md && grep -q "always_load: true" agents/context.md && grep -q "always_load: true" agents/repo.md

findings.title: The README describes the solution but not its success and scope decisions
findings.impact: Future changes have no durable answer for the primary problem, appetite, success outcome, nonfunctional thresholds, explicit no-gos, or assumptions, so feature and hardening decisions cannot be checked against an agreed product boundary.
findings.fix: Add a compact product brief for this weekend-scale local API covering problem and actor, appetite and cut rule, measurable success, NFR thresholds, no-gos, risks, and assumptions without inventing research.
findings.verify: grep -q "## Success" docs/product.md && grep -q "## Non-goals" docs/product.md && grep -q "## Assumptions" docs/product.md

findings.title: The embedded-service shape has no recorded ceiling or decision boundary
findings.impact: A future maintainer cannot tell when the synchronous single-process SQLite shape stops fitting or which alternatives were deliberately rejected.
findings.fix: Write one retroactive ADR naming the local single-process shape, numeric ceiling, latency and availability assumptions, rejected alternatives, flip point, and blast radius.
findings.verify: grep -q "Flip point:" docs/adr/001-local-service-shape.md && grep -q "Blast radius:" docs/adr/001-local-service-shape.md

findings.title: The dependency-free Node and SQLite choice has no decision record
findings.impact: The unusually constrained stack is clear in code but lacks a recorded pre-flight basis, rejected alternatives, scale ceiling, switching cost, date, and re-check trigger.
findings.fix: Add a compact stack decision that records why built-in Node HTTP and SQLite fit, which alternatives lost, the measurable flip point, switching cost, decision date, and review trigger.

findings.title: No executable style or quality budget protects the JavaScript dialect
findings.impact: The current naming and control-flow style is consistent, but new code has no formatter, linter, complexity budget, or single verification command preventing drift.
findings.fix: Add a zero-dependency-compatible formatting and lint strategy, scripts for format and lint checks, and explicit function-length and nesting budgets; document any choice to use Node built-ins instead of external tools.
findings.verify: npm run format:check && npm run lint

findings.title: The service has no structured operational boundary or health signal
findings.impact: Operators can only infer service health from ad-hoc console output, and there is no route that checks the SQLite dependency before reporting readiness.
findings.fix: Add a shared structured logger and a loopback-only /ready endpoint that performs a read-only database check and returns 503 on failure.

tasks.title: Enforce tenant isolation below the request handler
tasks.reuses: the existing organization-scoped store API and bidirectional isolation regression suite
acceptance: No notes table or note lookup can address records from more than one organization storage boundary.
The organization selector is derived only from the authenticated token and cannot be supplied by a request body or path.
The existing cross-organization GET, PATCH, DELETE, and list cases remain denied in both directions.
An ADR records the selected storage boundary, rejected alternatives, migration path, flip point, and blast radius.

tasks.title: Add atomic bearer-token rotation and revocation
tasks.reuses: hashToken, randomBytes(32), and the one-time token output convention
acceptance: A local administration command rotates exactly one user token in one transaction.
The replacement token is generated with 256 bits of randomness and printed once while only its hash is stored.
The previous token returns 401 immediately after rotation and the replacement token retains the same organization identity.

tasks.title: Apply authenticated-response hardening headers centrally
tasks.reuses: the json response helper as the single response-header enforcement point
acceptance: Every authenticated JSON response carries Cache-Control: no-store and X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff.
Every JSON response carries Referrer-Policy: no-referrer and a restrictive JSON-only content security policy.
Header assertions cover success and error responses without changing the existing error envelope.

tasks.title: Throttle failed authentication and mutations
tasks.reuses: the central app entry point before authenticate and the shared error envelope
acceptance: Failed authentication attempts are limited before the token lookup and return a documented 429 response.
POST, PATCH, and DELETE have bounded per-source request rates while normal loopback CRUD remains usable.
Limiter state is bounded and keys never retain raw bearer tokens.

tasks.title: Redact unexpected exceptions at the logging boundary
tasks.reuses: the injected logger seam already used by the API tests
acceptance: Unexpected request failures emit structured event code, error class, and correlation id without the raw message or stack.
Authorization values, token-shaped text, note bodies, and filesystem paths are redacted by one shared formatter.
The client continues to receive the generic INTERNAL_ERROR envelope.

tasks.title: Make note creation idempotent per organization
tasks.reuses: the existing SQLite transaction and organization-scoped store boundary
acceptance: POST /notes accepts a bounded Idempotency-Key and rejects malformed keys.
A unique organization-scoped key and its response are stored atomically with note creation.
Retrying the same key returns the original note without inserting a duplicate.

tasks.title: Add bounded cursor pagination to note lists
tasks.reuses: notes_organization_order and the existing createdAt-desc/id-asc ordering
acceptance: List requests enforce a documented maximum page size.
The cursor preserves created_at DESC and id ASC ordering without OFFSET.
Contract and API tests cover first page, next page, invalid cursor, and organization isolation.

tasks.title: Add SQLite backup and restore verification
tasks.reuses: DATA_FILE and SQLite WAL configuration documented by the current service
acceptance: The backup command uses a SQLite-safe online backup mechanism and never copies a live database file blindly.
README documents location, retention, WAL handling, and restore steps.
The restore verifier opens the copy, runs an integrity check, and confirms expected schema version.
tasks.verify: grep -q "Backup" README.md && test -x scripts/backup-sqlite.sh && test -x scripts/verify-restore.sh

tasks.title: Establish Git provenance and a network-free CI gate
tasks.reuses: npm test and the repository Node 22 engine pin
acceptance: The artifact has an identifiable initial Git commit and a protected default-branch workflow.
CI runs on pull requests and default-branch pushes with Node 22 and no outbound project network calls.
CI runs npm test plus audit artifact validation and contains no echo-only or soft-failed gate.
tasks.verify: git rev-parse --verify HEAD && grep -q "pull_request" .github/workflows/ci.yml && grep -q "npm test" .github/workflows/ci.yml

tasks.title: Add a Pillars loader and boundary-focused project memory
tasks.reuses: README, OpenAPI, and the existing auth, validation, and store boundaries as factual sources
acceptance: AGENTS.md implements the Pillars 1.1 six-step loader with structured project-specific exclusions.
context.md and repo.md are present always-load floor pillars within the size budgets.
API, auth, data, and quality pillars record tenant scoping, token handling, SQLite migration, and static-test invariants without duplicating inferable prose.
tasks.verify: grep -q "Pillars" AGENTS.md && grep -q "always_load: true" agents/context.md && grep -q "always_load: true" agents/repo.md

tasks.title: Write a compact product decision brief
tasks.reuses: the README job, organization role model, OpenAPI surface, and explicit offline constraint
acceptance: The brief states the problem and primary actor without inventing research.
It gives an appetite with a scope-cut rule and at most five measurable outcomes.
It records NFR thresholds, non-goals, risks, and assumptions with owned unknowns.
tasks.verify: grep -q "## Success" docs/product.md && grep -q "## Non-goals" docs/product.md && grep -q "## Assumptions" docs/product.md

tasks.title: Record the local service architecture decision
tasks.reuses: the current single-process Node, loopback, SQLite, and zero-runtime-dependency constraints
acceptance: The ADR is labeled retroactive and names a numeric scale ceiling plus NFR assumptions.
It records rejected alternatives, Flip point, and Blast radius.
The described deployable, storage, and trust boundaries match the current source.
tasks.verify: grep -q "Flip point:" docs/adr/001-local-service-shape.md && grep -q "Blast radius:" docs/adr/001-local-service-shape.md

tasks.title: Record the dependency-free stack decision
tasks.reuses: package.json, .nvmrc, README, and built-in node:sqlite usage
acceptance: The decision records pre-flight constraints, rejected alternatives, and its date.
It names a measurable Flip point, scale ceiling, and switching cost.
It includes a re-check trigger and does not claim external dependencies that are absent.

tasks.title: Wire formatting, lint, and maintainability budgets
tasks.reuses: the existing ESM package and named-export JavaScript dialect
acceptance: One formatter and one linter are configured with no duplicate tool per job.
package.json exposes format:check and lint commands.
Function length, nesting, unused code, and bare deferred-work markers have explicit budgets.
tasks.verify: npm run format:check && npm run lint

tasks.title: Add structured logging and truthful readiness
tasks.reuses: the app logger injection seam and a read-only SQLite prepared statement
acceptance: Boundary logs are structured with timestamp, level, event, service, and correlation id.
/ready checks SQLite through a read-only statement and returns 503 when the store is unavailable.
The readiness route remains loopback-only and is documented in OpenAPI.

tasks.title: Re-run godaudits at the remediated commit
tasks.reuses: the current check catalog, static capability, and evidence fingerprint process
acceptance: Every active remediation task is done and every linked finding is resolved with fresh evidence.
Fresh static evidence validates at the remediated Git commit.
AUDIT.json is compiled and AUDIT.mdx regenerated with score and coverage deltas recorded.

session_log.summary: Fresh full static audit; 18 modules reviewed; Critical/High findings refuted separately; no app, tests, network, or model calls run.

overall.grade_scope: an arithmetic roll-up of model-assigned pass/fail from a source read, not a test result, scan, or certification
